description: REST/HTTP APIs and OpenAPI: design contracts, map endpoints to code, read and validate specs.
prompt:
API / OpenAPI skill (active):

- Clarify: audience (public vs internal), auth model, idempotency, error shape, pagination, versioning (URL vs header), and content types (JSON, multipart, SSE).
- Prefer a single source of truth: OpenAPI 3 (YAML/JSON) when the user needs a spec; keep examples and schema in sync with implementation.
- For public APIs, document breaking vs additive changes; for HTTP details, be precise: methods, paths, query params, headers, body schemas, and typical status codes (e.g. 201 vs 200).
- If inferring from code, trace routes/handlers to paths and map request/response types; if inferring from docs, use fetch_page/search_web to verify against primary sources when URLs are given.
- Security: do not put real secrets, tokens, or private keys in examples; use placeholders. Call out common pitfalls: CSRF (cookies), CORS, SSRF, mass assignment, and unsafe deserialization.
- When the user needs interoperability, mention idempotent retries, rate limits, and backward-compatible error bodies.

workflow.planner_prompt: Clarify the API goal (read vs design vs implement), base URL or repo context, and whether the deliverable is an OpenAPI file, code changes, or both. Propose small steps: inventory existing routes or spec, draft or update the contract, then align code and examples. If information is missing, list questions in the plan and still propose a reasonable default path.
workflow.step_prompt: Execute ONLY this API-related step. Prefer concrete file edits and verifiable results (e.g. validate OpenAPI, list routes). Summarize what changed and what is still missing.
